commands.repair_reason: This security-labeled external PR needs contributor-owned real behavior proof and maintainer/security review, so ClawSweeper should not take over the repair lane.; Needs attention: The diff introduces a concrete security-boundary bypass by trusting raw marker substrings inside untrusted tool output.; Review finding: [P1] Do not trust raw boundary marker substrings — src/agents/transport-message-transform.ts:41-44 (sha=0bc55156df24c482078816939e3a4a4d52157dbb)
commands.review_summary:
This PR wraps text `toolResult` blocks in the shared agent transport transform with external-content markers and updates transport-message-transform tests for wrapped and pre-wrapped results.

PR surface: Source +57, Tests +70. Total +127 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR head: source inspection shows the marker-substring shortcut, and a reviewer comment includes harness output reproducing marker-injected tool output reaching the payload without a security notice. This is a PR-introduced path, not a current-main reproduction because current main does not include this wrapper.
commands.review_followup: This security-labeled external PR needs contributor-owned real behavior proof and maintainer/security review, so ClawSweeper should not take over the repair lane.

target.title: security: wrap tool results at transport boundary
target.body:
## Summary
- wrap tool-result text at the generic transport boundary using existing external-content security markers
- preserve already bounded tool results to avoid double-wrapping
- add regression coverage for wrapped and already-wrapped tool results

## Verification
- pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/transport-message-transform.test.ts
- pnpm exec tsc -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit --pretty false
- git diff --check
target.author: zeusbot-ron
